The loan is unsecured, has an annualized interest rate of <ix:nonFraction id="fid_776" contextRef="AsOf2022-04-08_aapi_ABAInvestmentGroupPtyLtdMember" name="us-gaap:InvestmentInterestRate" unitRef="Pure" decimals="2" scale="-2">3</ix:nonFraction>%, and is payable on demand by the Subsidiary Lender 2. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">d) On March 31, 2022, the Company entered into a loan agreement with Utti Pty Ltd (&#8220;Utti&#8221;). Utti is owned by a director of the Company. The loan is unsecured, bears interest at a rate of <ix:nonFraction id="fid_991" contextRef="AsOf2022-03-31_aapi_UttiOcoPtyMember" name="us-gaap:InvestmentInterestRate" unitRef="Pure" decimals="2" scale="-2">3</ix:nonFraction>%, and is payable upon demand. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">e) On March 31, 2022, the Company entered into a loan agreement with Mt. Wills Gold Mines Pty Ltd (&#8220;Mt. Wills&#8221;). The Company&#8217;s director is also a director and shareholder of Mt. Wills. The loan is unsecured, bears interest at a rate of <ix:nonFraction id="fid_992" contextRef="AsOf2022-03-31_aapi_MtWillsGoldMinesPtyMember" name="us-gaap:InvestmentInterestRate" unitRef="Pure" decimals="2" scale="-2">3</ix:nonFraction>%, and is payable upon demand. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">f) A director of the Company has advanced cash to the Company. The advances were unsecured and interest-free.</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td class="hpbhr">&#160;</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 1px solid; TEXT-INDENT: 0px;text-align:center;">F-13</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="page-break-after: always"></p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><em><a href="#tocf">Table of Contents</a></em></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1859" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31" name="us-gaap:DebtDisclosureTextBlock" escape="true"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>NOTE 6. LOAN PAYABLE</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On November 1, 2025, the Company entered into a Loan Agreement with PhilBook Pty Ltd(PhilBook Pty). PhillBook Pty agreed to loan the Company (AUD $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1880" contextRef="AsOf2025-11-01_aapi_PhilBookPtyLtdMember" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentFaceAmount" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="AUD" decimals="0">350,000</ix:nonFraction>). The loan is unsecured and has an <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1414" contextRef="AsOf2025-11-01_aapi_PhilBookPtyLtdMember" name="us-gaap:InvestmentInterestRate" unitRef="Pure" decimals="2" scale="-2">18</ix:nonFraction>% interest rate, payable upon demand. As of December 31, 2025, the Company drew $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1420" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31_aapi_PhilBookPtyLtdMember" name="aapi:BorrowedAmountOfLoan" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">206,782</ix:nonFraction> (AUD $309,836) on the loan, with a remaining $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1421" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31_aapi_PhilBookPtyLtdMember" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityFairValueOfAmountOutstanding" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">26,805</ix:nonFraction> (AUD $40,164) amount that can be borrowed under the loan. During the year ended December 31, 2025, the Company recorded $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1423" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31_aapi_PhilBookPtyLtdMember" name="us-gaap:InterestExpense" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">2,997</ix:nonFraction> in interest expense related to the loan. As of December 31, 2025, the Company has accrued $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1424" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31_aapi_PhilBookPtyLtdMember" name="us-gaap:AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">2,997</ix:nonFraction> of interest expense related to the loan.</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_427" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31" name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxDisclosureTextBlock" escape="true" continuedAt="cont_acd40a"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>NOTE 7.&#160;INCOME TAXES</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company utilized the asset and liability method of accounting for income taxes in accordance with FASB ASC 740-10. If it is more likely than not that some portion or all of a deferred tax asset will not be realized, a valuation allowance is recognized.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">a. United States (U.S.)</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company is subject to U.S. tax laws at a tax rate of <ix:nonFraction id="fid_419" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31_aapi_UnitedStatesMember" name="us-gaap:EffectiveIncomeTaxRateReconciliationAtFederalStatutoryIncomeTaxRate" unitRef="Pure" decimals="2" scale="-2">21</ix:nonFraction>%. No provision for US federal income taxes has been made as the Company had no taxable income for the years ended December 31, 2025, and 2024. The Company is subject to the State of Delaware tax laws at a tax rate of <ix:nonFraction id="fid_491" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31_aapi_UnitedStatesMember" name="us-gaap:EffectiveIncomeTaxRateReconciliationTaxSettlementsStateAndLocal" unitRef="Pure" decimals="3" scale="-2">8.7</ix:nonFraction>%.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">b. Australia (AU)</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Apple iSports Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of the Company, was incorporated in Australia in November 2021 and may be subject to a corporate income tax on its activities conducted in Australia and income arising in or from Australia. No provision for income tax has been made as the subsidiary had no taxable income for the years ended December 31, 2025, and 2024. The applicable statutory tax rate is <ix:nonFraction id="fid_420" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31_aapi_AustraliaMember" name="us-gaap:EffectiveIncomeTaxRateReconciliationForeignIncomeTaxRateDifferential" unitRef="Pure" decimals="2" scale="-2">25</ix:nonFraction>%.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company&#8217;s income tax returns are subject to the various tax authorities&#8217; examinations. The federal, state, and local authorities of the U.S. may examine the Company&#8217;s tax returns filed in the U.S. for three years from the date of filing. The Company&#8217;s U.S. income tax returns since 2019 are currently subject to examination.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Australian Taxation Office may examine the Company&#8217;s income tax returns filed in Australia for 4 years from the date of filing. The Company&#8217;s Australian subsidiary&#8217;s tax year end is currently June 30. The Company is planning on filing its tax return by the deadline on May 15, 2026.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Significant components of the Company&#8217;s net deferred income tax assets as of December 31, 2025, and 2024 consist of net operating loss carry forwards. The net operating loss carry forwards for U.S. federal tax and Australian tax purposes are available for carryforward indefinitely for use in offsetting taxable income. The U.S. federal net operating loss carry forward offset is limited to up to 80% of the taxable income. The State of Delaware net operating loss carry forwards are available for carry forward for 20 years for use in offsetting taxable income. Realization of the future tax benefits is dependent on the Company&#8217;s ability to generate sufficient taxable income within the carry-forward period.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">As of December 31, 2025 and 2024, the Company had total net operating loss carryforwards of approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_825" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31" name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">5,055,729</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_824" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31" name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">3,270,140</ix:nonFraction>, respectively, which consist of U.S. Federal and State (Delaware) net operating loss carryforwards of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_827" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31_aapi_UnitedStatesMember" name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">2,274,203</ix:nonFraction> and $<ix:nonFraction text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">In assessing the realization of deferred tax assets, management considers whether it is more likely than not that some portion or all of the deferred tax assets will not be realized. The ultimate realization of deferred tax assets is dependent upon the generation of future taxable income during the periods in which the net operating losses and temporary differences become deductible. Management considered projected future taxable income and tax planning strategies in making this assessment. The value of the deferred tax assets was offset by a valuation allowance due to the current uncertainty of the future realization of the deferred tax assets.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The timing and manner in which the Company can utilize operating loss carryforwards in any year may be limited by provisions of the Internal Revenue Code regarding changes in ownership of corporations. Such limitation may have an impact on the ultimate realization of its carryforwards and future tax deductions.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company follows FASB ASC 740.10, which provides guidance for the recognition and measurement of certain tax positions in an enterprise&#8217;s financial statements. Recognition involves a determination of whether it is more likely than not that a tax position will be sustained upon examination with the presumption that the tax position will be examined by the appropriate taxing authority having full knowledge of all relevant information.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company&#8217;s policy is to record interest and penalties associated with unrecognized tax benefits as additional income taxes in the statement of comprehensive income (loss). As of January 1, 2024, the Company had no unrecognized tax benefits and no charge during 2025, and accordingly, the Company did not recognize any interest or penalties during 2025 related to unrecognized tax benefits. There is no accrual for uncertain tax positions as of December 31, 2025.</p></ix:continuation><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_426" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31" name="us-gaap:StockholdersEquityNoteDisclosureTextBlock" escape="true" continuedAt="cont_1c6ae6"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>NOTE 8.&#160;STOCKHOLDERS&#8217; DEFICIT</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On May 5, 2022, the Company amended its Articles of Incorporation by filing a Certificate of Amendment with the Nevada Secretary of State which;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px 0px 0px 33.75pt; text-align:justify;">(a). Increased the authorized shares of common stock of the Company, par value $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1478" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31_aapi_ArticlesOfIncorporationMember" name="us-gaap:CommonStockParOrStatedValuePerShare" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">0.0001</ix:nonFraction>, from&#160;<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1483" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31_aapi_ArticlesOfIncorporationMember" name="us-gaap:CommonStockSharesAuthorized" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">200,000,000</ix:nonFraction>&#160;shares to&#160;<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1862" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31" name="us-gaap:CommonStockSharesAuthorized" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">500,000,000</ix:nonFraction>&#160;shares, and</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px 0px 0px 33.75pt; text-align:justify;">(b). Pursuant to the Common Stock Purchase Agreement, the Company is able issue and sell to the investor, and the investor shall purchase up to a maximum of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1475" contextRef="From2025-08-01to2025-08-04_aapi_CommonStockPurchaseAgreementMember" name="us-gaap:StockIssuedDuringPeriodSharesOther" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">25,000,000</ix:nonFraction> of the Company&#8217;s common stock, $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1443" contextRef="AsOf2025-08-04_aapi_CommonStockPurchaseAgreementMember" name="aapi:PurchaseOfStockPricePerShares" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">0.0001</ix:nonFraction> par value per share, subject to certain conditions therein. In addition, the Company delivered warrants to the investor to purchase up to <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1462" contextRef="From2025-08-01to2025-08-04_aapi_CommonStockPurchaseAgreementMember" name="aapi:CommonStockSharesPurchaseOfWarrants" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">7,692,492</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock at an initial exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1440" contextRef="AsOf2025-08-04_aapi_CommonStockPurchaseAgreementMember" name="us-gaap:SaleOfStockPricePerShare" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">7.76</ix:nonFraction> per share. The exercise price shall be adjusted on the six- and eighteen-month anniversary to the lower of the initial exercise price or a price equal to 110% of the average of the volume-weighted average price of the Company&#8217;s common stock over five trading days preceding each such date. The warrant&#8217;s expiration date is the third anniversary from the Effective Date.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company, from time to time, may submit draw down requests to the investor to purchase shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock at a prescribed purchase price. The amount of each draw down request shall not exceed 400% of the average daily trading volume for the 10 trading days immediately preceding a draw down request. The investor shall be obligated to accept the Company&#8217;s draw down notice, provided that the investor, in its sole discretion, shall not be obligated to accept more than 50% of the requested draw down amount and shall have the option to purchase up to 200% of the draw down amount requested. The price per share to be paid by the investor shall equal 90% of the average daily closing price during the pricing period for such draw down.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Pursuant to the Common Stock Purchase Agreement, the investor is entitled to a commission equal to 2% of the gross proceeds. The Company was not obligated to pay the commission during the year ended December 31, 2025 and recorded it as a&#160;deferred financing cost&#160;or issuance cost related to equity contract. As of December 31, 2025, there have been no share issuances of the Company&#8217;s common stock in connection with the Common Stock Purchase Agreement. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Regarding the warrants, the Company performed an analysis of the related provisions and concluded that the warrants met the guidance for being classified as an equity instrument. As the Company has not received any proceeds from share issuances under the Common Stock Purchase Agreement, the Company recognized the relative fair value of the warrant of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1463" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31_aapi_CommonStockPurchaseAgreementMember" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">1,740,042</ix:nonFraction> as a&#160;deferred financing cost&#160;or issuance cost related to equity contract upon issuance. The relative fair value of the warrant was determined using the Black-Scholes valuation model. As of December 31, 2025, there have been no warrants exercised or cancelled.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">As of the date of this filing, no transactions occurred with respect to the Common Stock Purchase Agreement. FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>NOTE 9.&#160;COMMITMENTS AND CONTINGENCIES</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">In the ordinary course of business, the Company or its subsidiaries may be named a party to claims and/or legal proceedings. As of December 31, 2025, we had a contingent liability totaling $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1869" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31" name="us-gaap:BusinessCombinationContingentConsiderationLiability" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">75,000</ix:nonFraction>, respectively, in accrued payroll on the consolidated balance sheets related to litigation matters, including those described below. We recorded an estimated charge of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1874" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31" name="us-gaap:LitigationReserve" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">75,000</ix:nonFraction> as of December 31, 2025 for litigation matters, in connection with the termination of Lee Seltzer. Mr. Seltzer made a demand on the Company for the sum of approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1875" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31_aapi_MrLeeSeltzerMember" name="us-gaap:LitigationSettlementGain" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">75,000</ix:nonFraction>, which he asserts is due under his employment agreement with the Australian subsidiary.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">As of December 31, 2025, the Company leased short term office spaces (<ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1876" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31" name="us-gaap:SaleLeasebackTransactionLeaseTerm" format="ixt-sec:durmonth">12</ix:nonNumeric> months or less), and as an accounting policy election, the Company has excluded all short-term leases from presentation on the balance sheet.</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1243" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31" name="us-gaap:DisclosureOfCompensationRelatedCostsShareBasedPaymentsTextBlock" escape="true" continuedAt="cont_58e0a1"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>NOTE 10. STOCK PLAN</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><em>Australian Plan</em></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On July 25, 2025, the Board of Directors adopted and approved the creation of a Stock Option Plan for Australian employees of the Company and its second-tier subsidiary. It also approved the stock option grant of a total of <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1531" contextRef="From2025-07-01to2025-07-25_aapi_USPlanMember" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsGrantsInPeriod" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">12,000,000</ix:nonFraction> shares under the 2025 Stock Plan, to certain Australian employees. The options have an exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1530" contextRef="From2025-07-01to2025-07-25_aapi_USPlanMember" name="aapi:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionExercisePrice" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">0.25</ix:nonFraction> per share. Under the stated plan, these options terminate immediately after the grantee&#8217;s cessation of employment or engagement with the Company.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">As of December 31, 2025 a total number of <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1591" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31_aapi_TwoThousandTwentyStockStockIncentivePlanMember" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAuthorized" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">60,000,000</ix:nonFraction> shares were authorized under the plan and <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1592" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31_aapi_TwoThousandTwentyStockStockIncentivePlanMember" name="us-gaap:DeferredCompensationArrangementWithIndividualCommonStockReservedForFutureIssuance" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">52,000,000</ix:nonFraction> shares were available for issuance. As of December 31, 2025, stock option grants totaling <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1532" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31_aapi_TwoThousandTwentyStockStockIncentivePlanMember" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsGrantsInPeriod" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">2,000,000</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock had a weighted average grant date fair value of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1528" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31_aapi_TwoThousandTwentyStockStockIncentivePlanMember" name="aapi:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionExercisePrice" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">0.25</ix:nonFraction> per share.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Compensation cost recorded for stock-based compensation awards (including awards to non-employee directors and consultants) reflected as a stock compensation expense was $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1526" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31_aapi_AustralianPlanMember" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">1,768,785</ix:nonFraction> for the year ended December 31, 2025. The maximum number of common stock authorized and available for issuance under the 2024 Stock Plan initially was <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1520" contextRef="AsOf2024-11-01" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAuthorized" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">15,000,000</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock. The options have an exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1529" contextRef="From2024-11-01to2024-11-02" name="aapi:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionExercisePrice" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">0.25</ix:nonFraction> per share, which was based on the subscription price of the Company&#8217;s then current private placement offering. In general, options become exercisable during the period when the grantee is providing services to the Company as an employee or consultant. Our policy is to issue new shares upon the exercise of options.&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On March 12, 2025, the Board approved an amendment of the Company&#8217;s 2024 Stock Incentive Plan to increase the shares issued under the plan from <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1521" contextRef="AsOf2025-03-12_aapi_MarchTwelveTwentyTwentyFiveMember_aapi_TwoThousandTwentyStockStockIncentivePlanMember" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAuthorized" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">15,000,000</ix:nonFraction> to <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1522" contextRef="AsOf2025-03-12_aapi_TwoThousandTwentyStockStockIncentivePlanMember" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAuthorized" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">20,000,000</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock.&#160;As of December 31, 2025 the Company authorized <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1593" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31_aapi_USPlanMember" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAuthorized" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">20,000,000</ix:nonFraction> shares under the plan and <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1595" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31_aapi_USPlanMember" name="us-gaap:DeferredCompensationArrangementWithIndividualCommonStockReservedForFutureIssuance" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">13,725,000</ix:nonFraction> shares available for issuance. As of December 31, 2025, stock option grants totaling <ix:nonFraction id="fid_1535" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31_aapi_USPlanMember" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsGrantsInPeriod" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="Shares" decimals="0">8,725,000</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock had a weighted average grant date fair value of $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1523" contextRef="AsOf2025-12-31" name="aapi:WeightedAverageGrantDateFairValueOfCommonStock" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USDPShares" decimals="INF">0.25</ix:nonFraction> per share.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Compensation cost recorded for stock-based compensation awards (including awards to non-employee directors and consultants) reflected as a stock compensation expense was $<ix:nonFraction id="fid_1527" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31_aapi_USPlanMember" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensation" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" unitRef="USD" decimals="0">2,566,695</ix:nonFraction> for the year ended December 31, 2025. As the awards were fully vested upon issuance, there is no unrecognized stock-based compensation expense as of December 31, 2025.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td class="hpbhr">&#160;</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 1px solid; TEXT-INDENT: 0px;text-align:center;">F-18</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="page-break-after: always"></p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><em><a href="#tocf">Table of Contents</a></em></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1244" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31" name="us-gaap:SegmentReportingDisclosureTextBlock" escape="true"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>NOTE 11.&#160;SEGMENT REPORTING</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Operating segments are defined as components of an enterprise with separate financial information, which are evaluated regularly by the chief operating decision maker (&#8220;CODM&#8221;) and are used in resource allocation and performance assessments. The Company&#8217;s Chief Executive Officer is the Company&#8217;s CODM. The Company is organized and operates as one operating and reportable segment that is developing a digital sports betting and gaming platform.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company&#8217;s CODM reviews financial information and operational forecasts presented on a consolidated basis for the purpose of making operating decisions and assessing financial performance. The Company&#8217;s CODM assesses performance for the Company&#8217;s single reportable segment based on the Company&#8217;s net income (loss) as reported on the consolidated statement of comprehensive income (loss).</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_698" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31" name="us-gaap:SubsequentEventsTextBlock" escape="true"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>NOTE 12.&#160;SUBSEQUENT EVENTS</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">We evaluated subsequent events after December 31, 2025 through the date of the issuance of these financial statements. Based on this evaluation, we identified the following subsequent events, from December 31, 2025 through the date the financial statements were issued. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On February 10, 2026, Lucky Bet informed the Company that it had terminated the referenced agreement due to the inability of the parties to reach an agreement on certain key terms of the agreement as well as certain other allegations regarding the agreement. As a result the Company wrote off the deposit made in Lucky Bet.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">On February 28, 2026, the Company entered into a Joint Venture Agreement (the &#8220;JV Agreement&#8221;) with AiC Enterprise LLC to supply gaming products platform and technical services to business enterprise and consumer clients. <ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1537" contextRef="From2026-02-01to2026-02-28_us-gaap_SubsequentEventMember" name="us-gaap:SubsequentEventDescription">This JV will be 50% owned by the Company and 50% owned by AiC Enterprise LLC</ix:nonNumeric>. AiC Enterprise LLC provides a robust global online crypto-enabled gaming platform, which is fully functioning and operational, a fully capable technological function suitable for deployment on a business enterprise basis, and an agentic AI driven marketing system.</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td class="hpbhr"/></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 1px solid; TEXT-INDENT: 0px;text-align:center;">F-19</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="page-break-after: always"></p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><em><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></em></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong><span class="atag" style="display: inline" id="i9">Item 9. Changes in and Disagreements with Accountants on Accounting and Financial Disclosure.</span></strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">There are not and have not been any disagreements between the Company and its accountants on any matter of accounting principles, practices, or financial statement disclosure.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong><span class="atag" style="display: inline" id="i9a">Item 9A. Controls and Procedures.</span></strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><span style="text-decoration:underline">Evaluation of Disclosure Controls and Procedures</span></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">In connection with the preparation of this annual report, an evaluation was carried out by the Company&#8217;s management, with the participation of the principal executive officer and the principal financial officer, of the effectiveness of the Company&#8217;s disclosure controls and procedures (as defined in Rules 13a-15(e) and 15d-15(e) under the Exchange Act (&#8220;Exchange Act&#8221;) as December 31, 2025. Disclosure controls and procedures are designed to ensure that information required to be disclosed in reports filed or submitted under the Exchange Act is recorded, processed, summarized, and reported within the time periods specified in the Commission&#8217;s rules and forms, and that such information is accumulated and communicated to management, including the principal executive officer and the principal financial officer, to allow timely decisions regarding required disclosures.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Based on that evaluation, the Company&#8217;s management concluded, as of the end of the period covered by this report, that the Company&#8217;s disclosure controls and procedures were not effective in recording, processing, summarizing, and reporting information required to be disclosed, within the time periods specified in the Commission&#8217;s rules and forms, and that such information was not accumulated and communicated to management, including the principal executive officer and the principal financial officer, to allow timely decisions regarding required disclosures.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><span style="text-decoration:underline">Management&#8217;s Report on Internal Control over Financial Reporting</span></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The management of the Company is responsible for establishing and maintaining adequate internal control over financial reporting. The Company&#8217;s internal control over financial reporting is a process, under the supervision of the principal executive officer and the principal financial officer, designed to provide reasonable assurance regarding the reliability of financial reporting and the preparation of the Company&#8217;s financial statements for external purposes in accordance with United States generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP). Internal control over financial reporting includes those policies and procedures that:</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font-size:10pt;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td style="width:4%;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td style="width:4%;vertical-align:top;"><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px"><span style="font-family:symbol">&#183;</span></p></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Pertain to the maintenance of records that, in reasonable detail, accurately and fairly reflect the transactions and dispositions of the Company&#8217;s assets;</p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px"><span style="font-family:symbol">&#183;</span></p></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Provide reasonable assurance that transactions are recorded as necessary to permit preparation of the financial statements in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles and that receipts and expenditures are being made only in accordance with authorizations of management and the board of directors; and</p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><p style="font-size:10pt;font-family:times new roman;margin:0px"><span style="font-family:symbol">&#183;</span></p></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Provide reasonable assurance regarding the prevention or timely detection of unauthorized acquisition, use, or disposition of the Company&#8217;s assets that could have a material effect on the financial statements.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Because of its inherent limitations, internal control over financial reporting may not prevent or detect misstatements. Also, projections of any evaluation of effectiveness in future periods are subject to the risk that controls may become inadequate because of changes in conditions or that the degree of compliance with the policies or procedures may deteriorate.&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company&#8217;s management assessed the effectiveness of our internal control over financial reporting as of December 31, 2025, based on criteria established in Internal Control-Integrated Framework issued by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission (2013), which assessment identified material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting. A material weakness is a control deficiency or a combination of deficiencies in internal control over financial reporting that creates a reasonable possibility that a material misstatement in annual or interim financial statements will not be prevented or detected on a timely basis. Since the assessment of the effectiveness of our internal control over financial reporting identified a material weakness, management considers its internal control over financial reporting to be ineffective.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Management has concluded that our internal control over financial reporting had the following material deficiencies:</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font-size:10pt;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td style="width:4%;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td style="width:4%;vertical-align:top;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><span style="font-family:symbol">&#183;</span></p></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Due to the absence of a sufficient system of overall internal controls over financial reporting, we were unable to maintain segregation of duties within our business operations due to our reliance on a single individual fulfilling the role of sole officer and director.</p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><span style="font-family:symbol">&#183;</span></p></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Lack of a functioning audit committee due to a lack of a majority of independent members and a lack of a majority of outside directors on our Board of Directors, resulting in ineffective oversight into the establishment and monitoring of required internal control and procedures.</p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><span style="font-family:symbol">&#183;</span></p></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Due to legacy issues with the Australian bank account, the (CODM) has required that the account be closed or its bank account number be changed to avoid repeated charges on that account from vendors. In addition, as part of the company&#8217;s financial control procedures, Mr. Marino Sussich will be the sole signatory on the account, with Cathy Verlench having transaction visibility.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160; </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td class="hpbhr">&#160;</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 1px solid; TEXT-INDENT: 0px;text-align:center;">31</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="page-break-after: always"></p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><em><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></em></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160; &#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">To the extent reasonably possible, given our limited resources, our goal is, upon consummation of a merger with a private operating company, to separate the responsibilities of the principal executive officer and principal financial officer, intending to rely on two or more individuals. We will also seek to expand our current board of directors to include additional individuals willing to perform directorial functions. Since the recited remedial actions will require that we hire or engage additional personnel, this material weakness may not be overcome in the near term due to our limited financial resources. Until such remedial actions can be realized, we will continue to rely on the advice of outside professionals and consultants.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">This annual report does not include an attestation report of our registered public accounting firm regarding our internal controls over financial reporting. Management&#8217;s report was not subject to attestation by our registered public accounting firm pursuant to Section 404(c) of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act, which permits us to provide only management&#8217;s report in this annual report.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Changes in Internal Controls over Financial Reporting</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">During the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, there has been no change in internal control over financial reporting that has materially affected or is reasonably likely to materially affect our internal control over financial reporting.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong><span class="atag" style="display: inline" id="i9b">Item 9B. Other Information.</span></strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Company has also corrected certain other identified rounding and immaterial errors that were identified during the impacted year.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong><span class="atag" style="display: inline" id="i9c">Item 9C. Disclosure Regarding Foreign Jurisdictions that Prevent Inspections </span></strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">None</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td class="hpbhr">&#160;</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 1px solid; TEXT-INDENT: 0px;text-align:center;">32</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="page-break-after: always"></p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><em><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></em></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong><span class="atag" style="display: inline" id="p3">PART III</span></strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong><span class="atag" style="display: inline" id="i10">Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers, and Corporate Governance.</span></strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The following table sets forth the name, age, and position of sole executive officers and directors. Executive officers are elected annually by our Board of Directors.&#160;&#160;Each executive officer holds his office until he resigns, is removed by the Board, or his successor is elected and qualified.&#160;&#160;Directors are elected annually by our stockholders at the annual meeting. Each director holds his office until his successor is elected and qualified or his earlier resignation or removal.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font-size:10pt;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: 1px solid;width:25%;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>NAME</strong></p></td><td style="width:1%;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: 1px solid;width:8%;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;"><strong>AGE</strong></p></td><td style="width:1%;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: 1px solid;"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>POSITION</strong></p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px;background-color:#cceeff"><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Joe Martinez</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;">73</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Chief Executive Officer, Chief Financial Officer, and Chairman</p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px;background-color:#ffffff"><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Marino Sussich </p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;">65</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Director</p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px;background-color:#cceeff"><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Lyndon Hsu</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:center;">60</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p></td><td><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Director</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Joe Martinez. </strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Mr. Martinez serves as Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of the Company. With a distinguished career spanning technology, investment banking, entrepreneurship, and venture capital, he brings deep operational expertise and strategic insight to his leadership role.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">He began his professional career in the technology sector, gaining extensive experience at Fortune 500 companies such as Xerox and IBM, as well as numerous Silicon Valley firms. He later transitioned into investment banking at Citigroup, where he leveraged his strong technological background to advise clients on complex transactions. Driven by entrepreneurial ambition, Mr. Martinez founded his own firm, which was subsequently acquired by a division of E*TRADE.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">He then served as CEO of a boutique investment bank in Palo Alto specializing in technology transactions and as a venture partner at a Palo Alto-based venture capital firm. In recent years, he founded Core Venture Partners, a firm that provides management consulting and financial advisory services to early-stage, middle-market, public, and private companies. He is also the co-founder and Chairman of Kiva Capital Group, LLC, which delivers tailored financial and operational services to Native American tribes across the United States and Canada.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">A decorated and disabled Vietnam War veteran, Mr. Martinez served as an infantry platoon leader. He holds graduate degrees from the University of Denver and Adams State University and completed the U.S. Army Infantry School&#8217;s (NCOC) program at Fort Benning, Georgia.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Marino Sussich</strong>.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Mr. Sussich is the founder of AiS and has served as a director of the Company since its inception. A serial entrepreneur with broad experience across multiple industries, Mr. Sussich has advised and built numerous companies throughout his career. He provides strategic consulting services to various businesses and owns a gold-mining asset in Australia. As a serial entrepreneur, he brings the discipline of his pilot&#8217;s license to business, where he has developed strong relationships with major investors in Hong Kong, Singapore, and Europe. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Since our Company&#8217;s inception, he has been instrumental to its growth and development, playing a pivotal role in capitalization efforts and serving as its principal funding source. Along with his financial background, his strategic vision for the company has been remarkable. To streamline the company, Mr. Sussich has taken on an operational role as President in addition to his director role and is the company&#8217;s leading funding source. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Lyndon Hsu.</strong> </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Mr. Hsu has served as a Director of the Company since December 2024. Mr. Hsu is a former global banker and financial executive, as well as an entrepreneur and investor with substantial experience in the gaming industry. Since July 2022, Mr. Hsu has been involved with several gaming-related companies based in Australia. In 2022, he served as Founding Chairman and a member of the Board of Directors of BetCloud Pty Ltd, a Melbourne-based wagering and data science company. Earlier in his career, Mr. Hsu held investment banking positions at Credit Suisse and HSBC. Mr. Hsu holds a Bachelor of Economics (Honours) from La Trobe University and a Master of Commerce (Finance) from the University of Melbourne.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">We will add two outside board members to the company when funding is complete and add staff to the U.S. operation as needed. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Family Relationship</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">There is no family relationship among the directors and officers of the Company.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Involvement in Certain Legal Proceedings</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">To the best of our knowledge, none of our directors or executive officers has been involved during the past ten years in any legal proceedings described in subparagraph (f)&#160;of Item 401 of Regulation S-K.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td class="hpbhr">&#160;</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 1px solid; TEXT-INDENT: 0px;text-align:center;">33</td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><p style="page-break-after: always"></p></td></tr><tr style="height:15px"><td><em><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></em></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Compliance with Section 16(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Section 16(a) of the Exchange Act requires the Company&#8217;s executive officers and directors and persons who own more than 10% of a registered class of the Company&#8217;s equity securities to file with the Securities and Exchange Commission (hereinafter referred to as the &#8220;Commission&#8221;) initial statements of beneficial ownership, reports of changes in ownership and annual reports concerning their ownership, of Common Stock and other equity securities of the Company on Forms 3, 4, and 5, respectively (&#8220;Section 16 Reporting&#8221;). Executive officers, directors and greater than 10% shareholders are required by Commission regulations to furnish the Company with copies of all Section 16(a) reports they file.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">During this fiscal year, the Section 16 Reporting was not complied with by the Company&#8217;s officers, directors and greater than 10% of shareholders. Compliance by such parties will be accomplished as soon as possible.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Significant Employees</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">We have no significant employees other than the officers and directors named in this Annual Report.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Code of Business Conduct and Code of Ethics</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">Our Board of Directors has not adopted a Code of Business Conduct and Ethics because we currently have only three individuals serving as our officer and director.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Nominating Committee</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">We have not adopted any procedures by which security holders may recommend nominees to our Board of Directors.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Audit and Compensation&#160;Committee</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The Board of Directors acts as the audit committee and compensation committee. The Company does not have a qualified financial expert at this time because it has not been able to hire a qualified candidate. Further, the Company believes that it has inadequate financial resources at this time to hire such an expert. The Company intends to continue to searching for a qualified individual to hire.</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Rule 10b5-1 Plans</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><ix:nonNumeric id="fid_1882" contextRef="From2025-01-01to2025-12-31" name="ecd:MtrlTermsOfTrdArrTextBlock" escape="true"><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">There are no 10b5-1 plans with respect to the Company&#8217;s employees, officers or directors and as a result the Company is not required to provide any disclosure regarding a 10b5-1 plan under Items 408(b) or 402(x) under Regulation S-K.</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong><span class="atag" style="display: inline" id="i11">Item 11. Executive Compensation.</span></strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>Executive Compensation </strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;"><strong>&#160;</strong></p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">The following table sets forth information concerning all cash and non-cash compensation awarded to, earned by, or paid to the named persons for services rendered in all capacities during the fiscal years ended December 31, 2025, and December 31, 2024. No other executive officer received a total annual salary and bonus compensation in excess of $100,000. </p><p style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman; MARGIN: 0px; text-align:justify;">&#160;</p><table style="border-spacing:0;text-align:left;font:10pt times new roman;width:100%" cellpadding="0"><tbody><tr style="height:15px"><td style="BORDER-BOTTOM: 1px
